claim: The unemployment rate fell to 3.9 percent in June
evidence: Bureau of Labor Statistics data shows the unemployment rate fell to 3.9 percent in June
Job growth remained steady across most sectors
label: supported

claim: The infrastructure bill allocates 550 billion dollars in new spending
evidence: The infrastructure bill allocates 550 billion dollars in new federal spending over five years
The bill funds roads, bridges, broadband and water systems
label: supported

claim: Crime increased by 40 percent last year in major cities
evidence: FBI statistics show violent crime in major cities increased by 12 percent last year
Property crime rates remained largely flat year over year
label: contradicted

claim: The senator voted for the healthcare bill
evidence: Voting records show the senator did not vote for the healthcare bill and was absent that day
The healthcare bill passed the chamber by a narrow margin
label: contradicted

claim: The new tariff policy will create two million manufacturing jobs
evidence: Trade policy affects employment through complex supply chain dynamics
Historical estimates of tariff employment effects vary widely between studies
label: unverifiable

claim: Voter turnout reached 66 percent in the presidential election
evidence: Election officials reported national voter turnout of 66 percent in the presidential election
Turnout was the highest recorded in over a century
label: supported

claim: The budget deficit doubled under the current administration
evidence: Treasury figures show the budget deficit grew by 35 percent over the period, not doubling
Deficit growth slowed in the most recent fiscal year
label: contradicted

claim: The education bill increases teacher salaries by 15 percent
evidence: The education bill increases minimum teacher salaries by 15 percent in participating states
Funding comes from a mix of federal grants and state matching
label: supported

claim: Foreign aid makes up 25 percent of the federal budget
evidence: Foreign aid accounts for roughly 1 percent of the federal budget according to official figures
Public surveys consistently overestimate the share of spending on foreign aid
label: contradicted

claim: The governor secretly met with lobbyists before signing the energy bill
evidence: Solar and wind installations grew rapidly last year
The energy bill was signed in a public ceremony in the capital
label: unverifiable

claim: Inflation was 2.4 percent in the last quarter
evidence: Consumer price index data shows inflation of 2.4 percent for the last quarter
Core inflation excluding food and energy was slightly lower
label: supported

claim: The immigration bill was never debated on the floor
evidence: Congressional records show the immigration bill was not debated on the floor before the recess
The bill remains in committee awaiting markup
label: supported
